description.en:
## Overview

Pak Nam Chumphon is the coastal fishing town at the mouth of the Tha Tapao River where it empties into the Gulf of Thailand, located about 10 kilometers southeast of Chumphon city center. This is a genuine working fishing port where daily life revolves around the tide, the catch, and the sea. Unlike tourist-oriented fishing villages elsewhere in Thailand, Pak Nam Chumphon remains thoroughly local, with waterfront seafood restaurants, a morning fish market, and colorful fishing boats lining the quay creating an atmosphere of authentic coastal Thai life.

## Seafood and Markets

The main draw for visitors is the seafood. Waterfront restaurants along the river serve dishes made from fish and shellfish that were swimming just hours earlier, at prices that reflect the lack of tourist markup. A whole steamed fish costs as little as 150 THB, and a full seafood spread for two rarely exceeds 500 THB. The morning fish market near the pier is worth an early visit to see the catch being sorted and sold, with vendors offering everything from squid and prawns to crabs and clams. The market is busiest between 6:00 and 8:00 AM when the boats return.

## River Estuary and Views

The river estuary where fresh water meets the sea creates an attractive setting with mangrove-lined banks and views across to the Gulf of Thailand. A riverside promenade offers pleasant walking in the late afternoon, and the area around the pier provides good vantage points for watching fishing boats come and go. The nearby Khao Matsee Viewpoint is easily accessible from Pak Nam for panoramic sunset views over the coast.

## Practical Information

Pak Nam Chumphon is accessible 24 hours with no entrance fee. The seafood restaurants are typically open from 10:00 AM to 9:00 PM, while the morning fish market runs from approximately 5:00 AM to 8:00 AM. Getting here from Chumphon city center takes about 15 minutes by car or motorbike. The town is also a departure point for some ferry services to Koh Tao, though the main Lomprayah pier is at Thung Makham Noi further south.
description.nl: Het vissershaven- en riviermonding-gebied van Chumphon waar de Tha Tapao rivier de Golf van Thailand ontmoet. Waterkant visrestaurants serveren de ochtendvangst voor lokale prijzen, vissersboten liggen langs de kade en de pier biedt verbindingen naar Koh Tao en andere Golfeilanden. Een werkende haven met authentiek karakter.

best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6-8 AM) for the fish market; late afternoon for riverside walks
tips: Visit the morning fish market between 6 and 8 AM to see the fresh catch being sorted and sold
Combine with a late afternoon trip to nearby Khao Matsee Viewpoint for sunset over the coast
A full seafood spread for two at the waterfront restaurants rarely exceeds 500 THB

metaDescription.en: Visit Pak Nam Chumphon. A working fishing port with fresh seafood restaurants and ferry connections to Koh Tao and the Gulf islands.
metaDescription.nl: Bezoek Pak Nam Chumphon. Een werkende vissershaven met verse visrestaurants en veerbootverbindingen naar Koh Tao en de Golfeilanden.
